title: Multiplication operator unsupported inside constraint expressions

features.name: simple multiplication constraint in std::randomize
features.description: Code should include std::randomize() with a constraint block using multiplication operator to compute a variable value
features.snippet:
int a, b, c;
if (std::randomize(a, b, c) with { a == 5; b == 3; c == a * b; } != 1) $stop;

features.name: multiplication constraint with range bounds
features.description: Code should include std::randomize() with range constraints and multiplication assignment in constraint block
features.snippet:
int x, y, z;
if (std::randomize(x, y, z) with { x >= 1; x <= 10; y >= 1; y <= 10; z == x * y; } != 1) $stop;

features.name: multiplication constraint with inside operator
features.description: Code should include std::randomize() with inside operator constraints and multiplication assignment
features.snippet:
int x, y, z;
if (std::randomize(x, y, z) with { x inside {[2:5]}; y inside {[3:6]}; z == x * y; } != 1) $stop;
